insight: Thermite Core was widely considered overpowered by top players in 2018, enabling a strategy of stacking Pixies while withholding attacks to deny opponent absorb entirely, with no real counterplay unlike Frost Brooders or Endotherm Kit since the damage is real rather than chill-based. The community workaround was to embargo it.
author: consensus
context: foxclear raised the concern and supergreyman confirmed multiple top players agreed; liadahlia noted most players were already embargoing it.

insight: Manticore is effectively a BBB unit: it is sometimes worth buying a third Blastforge to pump out all four Manticores on consecutive turns without wasting Blue or attacking for more than 1, because being able to spam Manticores consecutively lets you build economy while accumulating attack simultaneously.
author: .bky_1556
context: Discussion of a custom unit inspired by Manticore mechanics led bky to articulate this known strategic insight about optimal Manticore sequencing.

insight: Apollo can be used to snipe high-value targets such as Tia Threnody, Savior, and Infusion Grid, not just the usual low-HP units. Hitting Tia Threnody with Apollo is considered a highly punishing 'cheese' interaction that will likely cause the Tia player to lose even though the unit itself is not destroyed.
author: kaelii
context: Discussion of a custom unit with an Apollo-like snipe ability prompted a review of what high-value targets Apollo-style sniping can reach.

insight: When a large burst of attack (e.g., from a Redeemer-style unit producing multiple Gauss Charges simultaneously) is distributed across multiple build times instead of arriving all at once, it is actually harder for the opponent to absorb, since they cannot time defensive purchases like Iso Kronus to negate a single large spike.
author: kaelii
context: Debate over a proposed Redeemer rework that would spread out Gauss Charge build times instead of delivering all charges at once.

insight: Buying multiple Ampcos (Ampo) is sometimes done purely for damage, and sometimes specifically to fight Apollo — with the full set of four potentially acquired as an anti-Apollo measure rather than for their attack output alone.
author: masn6811
context: Brief exchange about how many Ampos to buy and for what purpose in the context of a custom unit discussion.

insight: Doomed Mech is considered the blue analogue of Grimbotch and Fission Turret in the design space of lifespan-based blocking units, with Fission Turret specifically trading one lifespan of blocking for Green that can be used defensively for Forcefields.
author: xyotsuba
context: Discussion of design space for lifespan attacker/blocker units across different tech colors.

insight: In Prismata, damage numbers on units are expected to always provide an accurate upper estimate of potential attack output; designs where the attack value is variable depending on board state (e.g., counting current Wall supply) violate this design principle because players cannot easily calculate maximum threat.
author: darkeisbein
context: Critique of a custom Thor unit whose click ability dealt damage equal to the number of Walls in play, making the attack number unpredictably variable.
